channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Government bails out Rex's multi-million-dollar debt to regional airports
item.description: Council-owned airports have been bailed out by the federal government to the tune of $4.8 million, $2 million of which was for NSW alone.
item.dc:creator: Jenae Madden and Lily Plass

media:group.media:description: Thirty-four councils across Australia have received part of a $4.8 million bailout, including Dubbo Regional Council which was owed $598,672.

item.title: Dog-friendly cafe issued warning after customer complaint
item.description: Kellie Hunter opened her cafe in the Adelaide Hills in 2023 as a friendly place for dogs and their owners, but the local council has now put a stop to it after one complaint.
item.dc:creator: Eugene Boisvert and Bobby Macumber

media:group.media:description: Same But Different Cafe owner Kellie Hunter can now only serve "puppacinos" outside.

item.title: Aged care bed shortage "substantial" and unavoidable, WA report finds
item.description: It's going to get much harder to find an available aged care bed in WA, a new report finds, with a "substantial and time-critical shortfall" predicted.
item.dc:creator: Keane Bourke

media:group.media:description: WA is facing a "time-critical" aged care bed shortage by 2030.

item.title: Mobile GP service and after-hours care to end because of Tasmanian health cuts
item.description: Primary care providers being forced to cut services after losing Tasmanian government funding say the result will be added strain on "pressure cooker" emergency departments, which could see them "explode".
item.dc:creator: Josh Duggan

media:group.media:description: An after-hours clinic in Cygnet will end after its funding was cut in last week's budget.

item.title: Remote residents call for more NT government support over diphtheria outbreak
item.description: Residents and service providers in the remote community of Yuendumu, where there are reports of dozens of diphtheria cases, say the NT government has not done enough to keep people safe.
item.dc:creator: Elsie Lange

media:group.media:description: Yuendumu residents have told the ABC there have been about 30 confirmed cases of diphtheria in the Central Australian community.

item.title: Parramatta council official edited applications to help friends get jobs
item.description: During her third day on the witness stand, Roxanne Thornton told the Independent Commission Against Corruption (ICAC) she was responsible for hiring processes that were "extremely flawed".
item.dc:creator: Nabil Al-Nashar

media:group.media:description: Roxanne Thornton told ICAC she edited job applications for friends to help them secure jobs with City of Parramatta Council.

item.title: Housing minister says Waterloo community 'not functional' as demolition looms
item.description: Rose Jackson made the comment on radio as workers began fencing off 150 public homes to be demolished to make way for a mixed-tenure development.
item.dc:creator: Declan Bowring

media:group.media:description: Activists interrupted efforts to fence off the first lot of public homes to be demolished.

item.title: Man jailed for fatally amputating man's foot using circular saw
item.description: Kalman Tal's loved ones have told of their distress at how the 66-year-old's leg was amputated in a public park. Farm worker John Yalu has today been sentenced for manslaughter.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Testa

media:group.media:description: John Yalu used a circular saw to amputate the 66-year-old's foot.

item.title: Police investigate suspicious death of man found in Alice Springs home
item.description: A 44-year-old man has been found dead in a home in Alice Springs under "suspicious" circumstances, with NT police launching an investigation into the incident.

media:group.media:description: NT police are treating the death of a 44-year-old man in Alice Spring as suspicious.

item.title: 'We're taking him home': Aboriginal stockman's remains repatriated
item.description: An Aboriginal stockman whose remains have been held by the South Australian Museum for six decades will this week be returned to Country in a remote national park.

media:group.media:description: The stockman's remains were kept at the SA Museum in Adelaide but will be reburied in Witjira National Park.

item.title: China launches astronauts into space amid new race to Moon
item.description: China has launched three astronauts into space, including one who will stay for a year for a study of the long-term impacts on a human in orbit, ahead of Beijing's goal of a crewed Moon landing by 2030.

media:group.media:description: China's Shenzhou-23 manned space mission was launched as part of a wider bid to study the long-term impacts of residing in space.

item.title: Gold medallist who endured 16 surgeries in 9 months hangs up oars
item.description: Paralympic gold medallist Nikki Ayers reflects on a career that saw her face ableism, homophobia and sexism.
item.dc:creator: Adriane Reardon and Eddie Williams

media:group.media:description: Nikki Ayers is a paralympic gold medallist.

item.title: Push for law change that could keep Baden-Clay behind bars for longer
item.description: The possible release of wife killer Gerard Baden-Clay next year could be pushed back by up to a decade under proposed changes to Queensland's parole laws.
item.dc:creator: Alex Brewster

media:group.media:description: Gerard Baden-Clay was sentenced to life in prison in 2012 for the murder of his wife, Allison, with a non-parole period of 15 years.

item.title: Live: Dezi Freeman goaded police to 'have a beer' before final shootout, court hears
item.description: The details of Dezi Freeman's initial confrontation with police and text messages he sent to his wife after shooting two police officers have been revealed in a hearing.
item.dc:creator: Judd Boaz and Melissa Brown

media:group.media:description: Dezi Freeman was on the run for seven months before he was shot by police on Monday.

item.title: Football Australia reeling as staff review reveals 'toxic, chaotic' culture
item.description: Football Australia employees are asked to use three words to describe the culture at the organisation, and the results are damning.

media:group.media:description: Football Australia says it is working to improve the workplace culture under new chief executive Martin Kugeler.

item.title: Magnussen flops at Enhanced Games, finishes last twice
item.description: James Magnussen, wearing a garish golden suit, has finished dead last at the inaugural Enhanced Games event.
item.dc:creator: Simon Smale

media:group.media:description: James Magnussen is taking performance-enhancing drugs.

item.title: Trump says a deal is close. Here are the sticking points still dividing the US and Iran
item.description: A possible US-Iran agreement is taking shape, but the toughest issues are still unsettled, including reopening the Strait of Hormuz, curbing Tehran's nuclear ambitions, and ending regional fighting.
item.dc:creator: Audrey Courty

media:group.media:description: Control over the Strait of Hormuz remains one of the biggest hurdles in US-Iran talks.

item.title: Did the Giants just end the Lions' AFL premiership hopes?
item.description: GWS stunned Brisbane and the AFL with a third-quarter goal avalanche in Sydney. But how did they do it and what remains of the Lions' hopes of a premiership threepeat?
item.dc:creator: Cody Atkinson and Sean Lawson

media:group.media:description: Callum Brown was one of eight multiple goalkickers for GWS as the Giants thrashed two-time reigning premiers Brisbane in Sydney.

item.title: Surfing event in NZ issued 'code red' after animal attacks photographer
item.description: The New Zealand Pro World Surf League is put on hold after what is believed to be a seal attacked a photographer.

media:group.media:description: A surfer is taken from the water after the apparent seal attack.

item.title: Mid-air plunge on Cathay flight leaves 10 injured, passengers shaken
item.description: An Australian passenger aboard a flight from Brisbane to Hong Kong says he thought he was going to die after the aircraft suddenly plunged mid-air.
item.dc:creator: Kristy Sexton-McGrath

media:group.media:description: Cathay Pacific flight in air.

item.title: Death of 'drongo': Are Aussie insults and swearwords dying out?
item.description: Classic Australian swearwords and insults are becoming less popular with younger generations. What does losing them mean for our culture?
item.dc:creator: Shalailah Medhora

media:group.media:description: Linguists have found that younger people use swearwords and insults in different ways than older people.

item.title: Turnbull says independent party could fill 'vacuum' in Australian politics
item.description: Independent MPs Zali Steggall, Allegra Spender and David Pocock have confirmed they are in conversations about a potential party of independents in response to the rising popularity of One Nation.
item.dc:creator: Holly Tregenza

media:group.media:description: Indepedent MPs Zali Steggall and Allegra Spender have confirmed they are open to forming an alternative party.

item.title: Gunmen were shot within seven minutes of Bondi attack, inquiry hears
item.description: The Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion has entered a critical phase, assessing the circumstances surrounding the Bondi terror attack.
item.dc:creator: Phoebe Pin

media:group.media:description: Eleven people were shot within 29 seconds of the terror attack starting at Bondi Beach on December 14.

item.title: Rider disqualified for headbutt, sprint teams call BS on controversial finish
item.description: The Italian rider headbutted Bob Donaldson off his bike in the final sprint, as race organisers made a drastic call on a finishing circuit described by riders as unsafe.
item.dc:creator: Simon Smale

item.title: How a container of hot chocolate put the spotlight on airport security
item.description: A security scare that shut down a Victorian airport last week was found to be a false alarm.
item.dc:creator: Judd Boaz

media:group.media:description: Security at Australian airports is under a spotlight following a high-profile false alarm last week in Victoria.
